reason: Auto-generated report (generate_quality_report.py) is internally consistent: 13 evaluated dims sum to avg 93.99; da_waiver applied on architecture (60); composite_score 91.92 reads from .sessi-work/gate4_result.json and is above the 85 release threshold. Per-FR Gate 1 scores match quality_manifest.json::gate_results.gate1.{FR-01..05} (96.94/94.22/99.66/99.36/95.92). CRG dead-code advisory lists 15 entries, all from .claude/workflows/*.js framework callbacks / parser / harness entry points — consistent with the '(advisory — verify before removing; framework callbacks / entrypoints can be false positives)' disclaimer. No critical/high/medium/low issues. Provenance pointers to 05-verification/{BASELINE,VERIFICATION_REPORT}.md present.

gaps.message: Composite score cited as 91.92 in QUALITY_REPORT.md:5 derives from .sessi-work/gate4_result.json (the finalize-gate patched source), while .methodology/gate4_result.json still holds the agent-raw 90.2222 — harness_cli.py:3201 patches the persistent copy on finalize but the file shows it was not refreshed. Both numbers are ≥85 (release threshold), so this is documentation/manifest drift rather than a blocker; recommend re-running finalize-gate --gate 4 --phase 6 to synchronise the persistent file, or add a note clarifying which file is authoritative.
